chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI High Court Appeal No.410 of 2023 M/s Continental Energy (Pvt.) Ltd. Versus Province of Sindh & others Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on CMA 5171/23 2. For orders on office objection a/w reply as at “A” 3. For orders on CMA 5172/23 4. For hearing of main case. 5. For orders on CMA 5173/23 Dated: 17.11.2023 Mr. Ahmed Ali Hussain for appellant. -.-.- Heard the counsel. Appellant in this High Court Appeal has impugned an order, which is reproduced as under:- “1. Urgency disposed of. 2-3. Notice to the alleged contemnors as well as defendants through all modes except publication for the next date of hearing. To come up on 28.11.2023.” The submission of the learned counsel for the appellant is that some time back order was passed for raising boundary wall around the land in question and the appellant’s claim is that perhaps the order was passed that under the supervision of the Nazir such work may be carried out. However, per learned counsel, it was not done because of some issues cropped up on site and police officials claimed to have not been cooperating with the Nazir and applications to that effect, such as application for contempt and other miscellaneous applications, are pending. The matter is already pending before learned Single Judge and fixed on 28.11.2023 and there is nothing in this appeal except seeking an order of mandatory nature, which is prayed for in this appeal, which we cannot pass at this stage when the matter is already fixed before learned Single Judge, which could pass appropriate orders, after hearing the parties, as deem fit and proper.

However, per learned counsel, it was not done because of some issues cropped up on site and police officials claimed to have not been cooperating with the Nazir and applications to that effect, such as application for contempt and other miscellaneous applications, are pending. The matter is already pending before learned Single Judge and fixed on 28.11.2023 and there is nothing in this appeal except seeking an order of mandatory nature, which is prayed for in this appeal, which we cannot pass at this stage when the matter is already fixed before learned Single Judge, which could pass appropriate orders, after hearing the parties, as deem fit and proper. In these circumstances instant High Court Appeal merits no consideration and is accordingly dismissed along with listed applications. Judge Judge
